title: Kafka Table engine consuming Avro Confluent message containing union data type raises error
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
I'm consuming Kafka messages (Avro Confluent format) using the Kafka table engine from different kind of topics  and works well but when trying to consume a topic that contains a 'union' Avro data type as follows the kafka consumer raises the error: "The number of leaves in record doesn't match the number of elements in tuple: column payload: (at row 1)"
{\"name\":\"attributes\",
\"type\":[\"null\",{\"type\":\"map\",\"values\":[\"null\",\"string\",\"boolean\",\"int\",\"long\",\"float\",\"double\"]}]}
The clickhouse data schema inference mechanism define this data type as:  **attributes Map(String, Variant(Bool, Float32, Float64, Int32, Int64, String))**
This data type requires the activation of the setting "allow_suspicious_variant_types = 1"
When loading messages directly from file it works well. When loading using Kafka table engine raises the mentioned error.
I am doing something wrong? missing something?
Thanks
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.8.9.20
### How to reproduce
1. Inference of data model:
`CREATE TABLE STREAMS.tests_raw (raw String)
ENGINE = Kafka
SETTINGS kafka_broker_list = 'localhost:9092',
kafka_topic_list = 'test.document.public.v1',
kafka_group_name = 'testing',
kafka_format = 'RawBLOB';`
`INSERT INTO FUNCTION file('./avro_raw_document.avro', 'RawBLOB')
SELECT * FROM STREAMS.tests_raw LIMIT 1
SETTINGS max_block_size=1, stream_like_engine_allow_direct_select=1;`
`CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE document_test AS
SELECT * FROM file('./avro_raw_document.avro', 'AvroConfluent')
SETTINGS format_avro_schema_registry_url='localhost:8081'
SETTINGS allow_suspicious_variant_types = 1;`
`SHOW CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE document_test\G;`
2. Create Kafka engine table using the inferred data model  as follows:
SETTINGS kafka_broker_list = 'localhost:9092',
kafka_topic_list = 'test.document.public.v1',
kafka_group_name = 'testing',
kafka_format = 'AvroConfluent',
format_avro_schema_registry_url = 'localhost:8081',
kafka_handle_error_mode='stream',
allow_suspicious_variant_types = 1,
allow_experimental_variant_type = 1,
input_format_avro_allow_missing_fields = 1;
4. Create MergeTree destination table using the same inferred data model.
5. Create the MV as "Select * from".
6. Create the dlq MV.
7. Show dlq MV errors.
### Expected behavior
Documentation shows that this data conversion is supported : https://clickhouse.com/docs/interfaces/formats/AvroConfluent#data-type-mapping
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_

         kafka_topic_list = 'test.document.public.v1',

         kafka_format = 'RawBLOB';
key_bug_features: Kafka table engine consuming union Avro data type
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS kafka_test (id UInt32, attributes Map(String, Variant(Bool, Float32, Float64, Int32, Int64, String))) ENGINE = Kafka SETTINGS kafka_broker_list = 'localhost:9092', kafka_topic_list = 'test.document.public.v1', kafka_group_name = 'testing', kafka_format = 'AvroConfluent', format_avro_schema_registry_url = 'localhost:8081', allow_suspicious_variant_types = 1;
INSERT INTO kafka_test (id, attributes) VALUES (1, {'key1': 1.5, 'key2': true, 'key3': 'value'});
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS processed_data AS SELECT id, attributes['key1'] AS key1_value FROM kafka_test WHERE attributes['key2'] = true;
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS error_log (error_message String, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now()) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY created_at;
INSERT INTO error_log (error_message) SELECT 'Error processing message' WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM processed_data WHERE key1_value IS NOT NULL);
